Package global.thalion.ttio.protocols

Interface Run

All Known Implementing Classes:
AcquisitionRun, GenomicRun
public interface Run
Modality-agnostic surface shared by every run type — mass-spectrometry, NMR, FID, MSImage, and genomic. A "run" is a sequence of measurements (spectra in the MS / NMR / FID case, aligned reads in the genomic case) that share an acquisition mode, instrument context, and provenance chain.

Code that wants to operate uniformly on either modality should type-hint against Run and use only the methods listed below. Modality-specific work (e.g. extracting a CIGAR string from an aligned read, or a precursor m/z from a mass spectrum) requires narrowing via instanceof to the concrete class.

Both AcquisitionRun and GenomicRun implement this interface. Java is nominal-typed so explicit implements is required (unlike Python's structural Protocol).

API status: Provisional (Phase 1 abstraction polish, post-M91).

Cross-language equivalents: Objective-C TTIORun, Python ttio.protocols.run.Run.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Enums.AcquisitionMode
    acquisitionMode()
    Acquisition mode enum value identifying the instrument / protocol context.
    int
    count()
    Number of measurements in the run.
    Object
    get(int index)
    Return the i-th measurement.
    String
    name()
    Run identifier as stored in the .tio file (e.g.
    List<ProvenanceRecord>
    provenanceChain()
    Per-run provenance records in insertion order.

  • Method Details

    • name

      String name()
      Run identifier as stored in the .tio file (e.g. "run_0001" or "genomic_0001").

    • acquisitionMode

      Enums.AcquisitionMode acquisitionMode()
      Acquisition mode enum value identifying the instrument / protocol context.

    • count

      int count()
      Number of measurements in the run. Modality-specific: spectra for AcquisitionRun, aligned reads for GenomicRun.

    • get

      Object get(int index)
      Return the i-th measurement. The element type is modality- specific (Spectrum / AlignedRead) — return type is Object because Java has no covariant generic abstraction over the two element families.

      Implementations should raise IndexOutOfBoundsException on out-of-bounds indices.

    • provenanceChain

      List<ProvenanceRecord> provenanceChain()
      Per-run provenance records in insertion order. Empty list when the run has no provenance attached.
